message:
br-t62hq: enforce timeout_secs>=1 for wa.wait_for + wa.send at call site

ft-t62hq: WaWaitForTool (mcp_tools.rs:1085) and WaSendTool (1771) each
advertise input_schema with `"timeout_secs": { "type": "integer",
"minimum": 1, "default": 30 }` — but WaitForParams.timeout_secs and
SendParams.timeout_secs are plain u64, which serde_json::from_value
happily accepts 0 for. No post-parse bounds check existed at either
call site.

Silent-failure path without the fix:
  1. Client sends { pane_id: 1, pattern: "OK", timeout_secs: 0 }.
  2. serde_json::from_value::<WaitForParams> returns Ok (u64 accepts 0).
  3. Duration::from_secs(0) produces a zero-duration timeout.
  4. The wait loop returns matched: false, polls: 0, elapsed_ms: 0
     — immediately, even when the pattern would have appeared microseconds
     later. Clients that type-coerce (JS Math.floor, CLI "no timeout"
     flags that pass 0) land on this silently.

Same gap in wa.send — the wait_for stage becomes an instant no-op when
timeout_secs == 0 despite the schema declaring minimum: 1.

Fix: add a post-parse validation block at each call site that returns
MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with a message pointing at the schema's minimum.
Pattern matches the wa.events limit-enforcement block at line 1638,
whose comment already warned: "many MCP clients don't validate tool
inputs against the schema before sending — a malicious or buggy caller
can otherwise send limit: 0 (silent no-op)..." — identical class of
bug for timeout_secs.

The same mismatch (u64 struct, schema minimum: 1, maximum: 600) exists
for wa.cass_search / wa.cass_view / wa.cass_status at lines 511 / 621
/ 711; they're scoped out of this bead so the fix stays reviewable.
Tracked for a follow-up sweep.

cargo check -p frankenterm-core --tests --offline --features mcp on
/tmp/ft-pane1-target passes clean in 2m12s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
